front: What is the role of amyloid-β (Aβ) peptide in Alzheimer's disease?
back: Aβ peptides (38-43 aa) are cleaved from amyloid precursor protein (APP) by β-secretase and γ-secretase. Aβ42 is particularly prone to aggregation, forming oligomers and fibrils that cause neurotoxicity, synaptic dysfunction, and plaques in the brain.
